L1.area: Sandbox file system access and isolation layer

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists in the remote sandbox file reading mechanism where path validation and the actual file read operation are performed as separate, non-atomic steps. An attacker can exploit the time gap between the path safety check and the file read to swap the target, escaping the sandbox boundary and reading arbitrary files on the host.

L3.area: Remote shell-based sandbox filesystem bridge and its file reading logic
L3.description: A TOCTOU (time-of-check time-of-use) race condition exists in the remote sandbox file read path where the path is first resolved and validated for safety, then the file is read in a separate shell command using the resolved path. Between the validation and the read, the file target can be swapped via symlink manipulation, allowing sandbox escape and unauthorized file access on the remote host.

ground_truth.reason: The remote filesystem bridge performs path resolution and safety checks separately from the actual file read, creating a TOCTOU window where an attacker can swap the validated path to escape the sandbox and read arbitrary host files.
